tasks.description:

**Deliverable:**

[https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-77517](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-77517)

22\. PER Test with FG with AGC with Start Sequence Robustness
**Measured Parameters:** Read the packet bytes from Rx FIFO once reception is done and QS\_MCU\_IRQ\_1 received. Repeat for multiple packets.
**Expectation:** The read data will be the same as the write data.

1. QS\_TX\_OUT\_ANA | 20M Carrier | AGC enabled | SS Robustness enabled: [schdlr\_txrx\_agc\_ss\_robustness\_20m\_carrier\_cfg.json](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/shovan/IQDmESn1NYp3SZ03gxAY7uqxAanogsErfDMV516wTb-sQyo?e=8VtmlV)
### **Test operation**
Refer to this page: FW PER Replication TB for Simulation ([https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-21857/8cqezuv-66937](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-21857/8cqezuv-66937))

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] RSSI Plot with and without RSSI offset correction in the schedule for packet sizes of 63,127 and 255 bytes.
tasks.description:
Why: Ixana plans to take QS127M into production.

Deliverable / Description :

## **Test operation**
For **RSSI Offset Correction steps**, check this page: RSSI Offset Correction ([https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-76777](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-76777))
**Refer to this:**[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-75137?block=block-95a30a23-8df6-4a74-b1d7-9481ceccf6e3)
1. Power on the chip in normal mode (**QS\_MCU\_IO1\_BYP\_PS**\=0V) and write configs
2. To start schedule operation:
    1. **`ps_scheduler_start`** = 0➝1 <addr 0x0208\[5\]>
3. Also need to configure the PS descriptors and ADR bits separately as they are not configured by preset
4. Follow schedule test flow
5. At the end of every RX slot, check the following status registers:
    1. **`rssi_val_latched`** <addr {0x0445\[1:0\], 0x0444}>
    2. **`rssi_cal_done_latched`** <addr 0x0445\[2\]>
    3. **`rssi_baseline_val_latched`** <addr {0x0447\[1:0\], 0x0446}>
    4. **`rssi_cal_done_baseline_latched`** <addr 0x0447\[2\]>
6. Config **`ps_cfg_rssi_mode`** <addr 0x0219\[6\]> should be 1 for this again.
    1. NOTE that the RSSI will only trigger on the descriptor index set in **`ps_cfg_rssi_desc`** <addr 0x021A\[3:0\]> and RSSI baseline on the descriptor index set in **`ps_cfg_rssi_baseline_desc`** <addr 0x021D\[6:3\]>
        1. this does mean that if there are two back-to-back slots which use the same descriptor, it may overwrite
        2. see also **`ps_rssi_cal_value_update_slow`** <addr 0x0219\[1\]>
### RSSI Schedule Timings
**Refer to this:** [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/shovan/IQDk13gbXwq5RK1SrbgZRVNmAXqLtO7MmS6ePg1elJLK\_NI?e=VVOJZF](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/shovan/IQDk13gbXwq5RK1SrbgZRVNmAXqLtO7MmS6ePg1elJLK_NI?e=VVOJZF)
### Debug Signals
1. To bring out internal RSSI signals, write the following using SPI:
    1. **`ps_debug_sel`** **\=** 0x2.
    2. **`sel_debug_mux0`** = 0xF
        1. **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT0**: rssi\_en
    3. **`sel_debug_mux1`** \= 0xF
        1. **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT1**: rssi\_peak\_reset
    4. **`sel_debug_mux2`** \= 0xF
        1. **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT2**: rssi\_cal\_en
2. Note: Make sure **`ie_dbg_0`** & **`ie_dbg_1`** are set to 0 for output at **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT0** & **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT1** respectively.

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] Generating RSSI calibration curves with and without RSSI offset correction in non-schedule
tasks.description:
Why; Ixana plans to take QS127M to production.

Deliverable / Description:
## **Test operation**
For **RSSI Offset Correction steps**, check this page: RSSI Offset Correction ([https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-76777](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-76777))
### For PER test
1. Power on the chip in normal mode (**QS\_MCU\_IO1\_BYP\_PS**\=0V) and write configs
2. Send packet from Tx device with same datarate and carrier frequency as set for Rx
3. Wait for packet reception. When reception is done and packet is successfully received, **QS\_MCU\_IRQ\_1** pin will be asserted
4. [Read/Clear IRQ1](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-83537?block=block-c1b9e8cf-8453-4cbd-a896-57ad4ac79ffc)
5. [Packet read](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-83537?block=block-050738c1-abcb-4216-95cb-82a72b39e4c6) from Rx FIFO
6. Read status registers
    1. **`rssi_val_to_status_ps`** <addr {0x044A\[1:0\], 0x0449}>
    2. **`rssi_cal_done_to_status_ps`** <addr 0x044A\[2\]>
    3. **`rssi_value_baseline_to_status`** <addr {0x044C\[1:0\], 0x044B}>
    4. **`rssi_cal_done_to_status_baseline`** <addr 0x044B\[2\]>
7. When running in **QS\_MCU\_IO1\_BYP\_PS**\=0, the only bit that controls enabling is **`ps_cfg_rssi_mode`** =1 <addr 0x0219\[6\]>
    1. Note that if this bit is 0 it does not guarantee RSSI block is off, just that it will not be automatically controlled by PS
### For Schedule Tests
**Refer to this:**[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-75137?block=block-95a30a23-8df6-4a74-b1d7-9481ceccf6e3)
1. Power on the chip in normal mode (**QS\_MCU\_IO1\_BYP\_PS**\=0V) and write configs
2. To start schedule operation:
    1. **`ps_scheduler_start`** = 0➝1 <addr 0x0208\[5\]>
3. Also need to configure the PS descriptors and ADR bits separately as they are not configured by preset
4. Follow schedule test flow
5. At the end of every RX slot, check the following status registers:
    1. **`rssi_val_latched`** <addr {0x0445\[1:0\], 0x0444}>
    2. **`rssi_cal_done_latched`** <addr 0x0445\[2\]>
    3. **`rssi_baseline_val_latched`** <addr {0x0447\[1:0\], 0x0446}>
    4. **`rssi_cal_done_baseline_latched`** <addr 0x0447\[2\]>
6. Config **`ps_cfg_rssi_mode`** <addr 0x0219\[6\]> should be 1 for this again.
    1. NOTE that the RSSI will only trigger on the descriptor index set in **`ps_cfg_rssi_desc`** <addr 0x021A\[3:0\]> and RSSI baseline on the descriptor index set in **`ps_cfg_rssi_baseline_desc`** <addr 0x021D\[6:3\]>
        1. this does mean that if there are two back-to-back slots which use the same descriptor, it may overwrite
        2. see also **`ps_rssi_cal_value_update_slow`** <addr 0x0219\[1\]>

### RSSI Manual Measurement **(FW Implementation)**:
**Refer to this doc:** Manual RSSI Measurement ([https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-76797](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-76797))
Find the JSON reg settings in the linked page.
### RSSI Schedule Timings
**Refer to this:** [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/shovan/IQDk13gbXwq5RK1SrbgZRVNmAXqLtO7MmS6ePg1elJLK\_NI?e=VVOJZF](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/p/shovan/IQDk13gbXwq5RK1SrbgZRVNmAXqLtO7MmS6ePg1elJLK_NI?e=VVOJZF)
### Debug Signals
1. To bring out internal RSSI signals, write the following using SPI:
    1. **`ps_debug_sel`** **\=** 0x2.
    2. **`sel_debug_mux0`** = 0xF
        1. **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT0**: rssi\_en
    3. **`sel_debug_mux1`** \= 0xF
        1. **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT1**: rssi\_peak\_reset
    4. **`sel_debug_mux2`** \= 0xF
        1. **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT2**: rssi\_cal\_en
2. Note: Make sure **`ie_dbg_0`** & **`ie_dbg_1`** are set to 0 for output at **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT0** & **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT1** respectively.

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] PLL test using external reference clock.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana plans to take QS127M into production. 

**Deliverable:** 
**Measured Parameters:** Measure the Frequency offset and jitter at the RX PLL clock.
**Expectation:** Frequency of the observed clock at **QS\_DIG\_DBG\_OUT0** port will be 1.25MHz. The jitter and observed frequency (with frequency offset) values are given in this doc: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/p/shovan/IQAjcMgSgIeBT7mS8f\_SbgUHAXljcDwCX2x8fjdoXT6C1CE?e=QFRXW9](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/p/shovan/IQAjcMgSgIeBT7mS8f_SbgUHAXljcDwCX2x8fjdoXT6C1CE?e=QFRXW9)(Refer slide no. 15).

Detailed description of the test is documented here: See #14 PLL test using external reference.
[https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-74597](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-74597)

Risks (T,E):

Limitations (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency (D))

Results

comments.text:
Hi @Sarat Anumula, this task couldn't be targeted today due to the TX carrier measurements task. As we defined the FFT captures and the drive strength sweep for the TX task only on Saturday evening, test cases have increased by more than 80x - across 7 different drive strengths, across 4 carriers across 3 Vbat voltages for both FFT and signal capture.

As the TX test data is important for the wearables and regulatory testing, I will come back to this Flash PLL ADC/FIFO read-write task once after the TX carrier task is complete

Hence, can you please change the due date to Thursday. Thank you.
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath
